what:
Correct all stale references to the nonexistent `COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES` constant in the codebase's code comments and README. The command-policy configuration was refactored so entries are now defined inline as the `entries` array passed to `createCommandPolicyExtension({ entries: [...] })` in `packages/agent-lord/extensions/command-policy/index.ts`; there is no `COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES` constant and no `logic.ts` in that extension. Update the stale mentions in:
- `packages/agent-lord/extensions/fix-ci.ts` (header comment: "COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES bans the 'git push' subcommand")
- `packages/agent-lord/extensions/git-commit/index.ts` (header comment: "COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES bans the 'git commit' subcommand")
- `packages/agent-lord/extensions/command-policy/predicates.ts` (comment: "this extension's COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES bans")
- `packages/agent-lord/extensions/command-policy/predicates.test.ts` (comment: "used to build COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES")
- `README.md` line ~150 (tree annotation: "Wires COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES into @vt-pi/command-policy")

Each should describe the current reality: the command-policy extension's inline `entries` array (in `index.ts`) that bans/allows the relevant commands.

Note: the two docs that also reference `COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES` — `.pi/skills/command-policy/SKILL.md` and the repo `AGENTS.md` — are already addressed by an existing `plan.json`, so this change should focus on the code comments and README to avoid overlap.
why: `COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES` is a dead identifier: the policy entries were moved to an inline `entries` array passed to `createCommandPolicyExtension` in `packages/agent-lord/extensions/command-policy/index.ts`, and no such constant (nor a `logic.ts` in that extension) exists anymore. The lingering references mislead anyone reading the code or README into hunting for a constant or file that isn't there, and contradict the actual structure. Correcting them keeps comments and docs trustworthy and consistent with the code.

tasks.title: Update stale COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES references in code comments and README
tasks.goal: Replace every mention of the nonexistent `COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES` constant in code comments and the README with an accurate description of the current inline `entries` array passed to `createCommandPolicyExtension` in packages/agent-lord/extensions/command-policy/index.ts, so comments and docs match the code.
tasks.acceptance: `grep -rn COMMAND_POLICY_ENTRIES packages README.md` returns no matches (the SKILL.md and repo AGENTS.md references are handled separately). The updated comments in fix-ci.ts and git-commit/index.ts correctly state that git push / git commit are blocked by the command-policy extension's entries; predicates.ts and predicates.test.ts comments refer to the extension's policy entries rather than a constant; the README tree annotation describes wiring the inline entries into @vt-pi/command-policy. nix build still passes.
tasks.constraints: Do not modify .pi/skills/command-policy/SKILL.md or the repo-root AGENTS.md (covered by an existing plan). Only change comments/documentation text — no behavioral code changes. Keep wording consistent with the actual index.ts structure.
